items.headline: Washington lifts blanket asylum freeze for most nationalities
items.body: United States immigration authorities ended a total hold on asylum decisions affecting millions of applications, though the pause continues for nationals from 40 countries designated high-risk. The shift follows months of frozen processing after a National Guard shooting blamed on vetting failures.

items.headline: Gang warfare leaves dozens dead in central Haiti
items.body: Armed group Gran Grif attacked the town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite, scattering bodies across streets and burning homes in conflict with vigilante forces. The massacre adds to five years of spiraling violence that has displaced over seven million Haitians since the president's assassination.

items.headline: Italian voters block government judicial overhaul
items.body: Citizens rejected constitutional changes to separate judges from prosecutors by 54 to 46 percent in a referendum widely seen as a test of leadership. Premier Meloni conceded defeat but pledged to complete her mandate despite the setback one year before national elections.

meanwhile.text: Kaori Sakamoto won figure skating world championship gold in final performance before retirement
Scientists created first lab-grown esophagus that restores swallowing function in animals without immunosuppression
Harvard researcher claims aging reversal therapies could show first human evidence within months
Anti-establishment pragmatism emerges as dominant political worldview across ideological divides in America
